levels.body: “[D]urst” is the past tense form of the verb “to dare” ("dare, *v*.1" Inflections).
worksCited: "dare, *v*.1" *OED Online*, Oxford University Press, December 2021, www.oed.com/view/Entry/47273. Accessed 9 February 2022.

levels.body: The duke finds that the visitors always seem curious about the duchess’s looks but they would never risk to ask because they sense that they might publicly point to a hidden indecent story. The duke himself suggests that the visitors were intimidated by him, afraid that they would provoke him if they insinuated something improper about his duchess.
